shared_properties: Platform width stays 48 px and height stays 16 px to match the GDD collision footprint.
Surface trim uses moss and stone colors so the platform reads as part of the prison structure rather than a sci-fi device.
differences: This sheet adds a two-frame moss sway loop while preserving a hard top collision edge and a clean bottom silhouette for motion readability.

reference_images.label: Slip Down GDD mirror
reference_images.purpose: Platform size, 60 px per second motion rule, and moss presentation grounding.

reference_images.label: Slip Down moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Stone slab wear and damp moss texture grounding.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use TextureAtlasLayout with a short loop while gameplay code drives a horizontal sine or ping-pong translation over 80 px.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/moving_platform_sheet.png through AssetServer and keep the handle in the moving-platform visual recipe keyed by asset_key.
runtime_notes: Animation loop uses two frames at 4 FPS.
Pivot sits at the platform center so horizontal motion stays symmetrical.

context.role: Animated support platform that carries the player sideways during later descent bands.
context.usage: Appears from the water band onward to create route changes and timing variation.

deliverables.notes: Two-frame 48x16 platform loop for the moss sway animation.
Single move_loop clip definition for the moving platform.

acceptance_criteria: Sheet size is exactly 96x16 px with two 48x16 frames.
Top collision edge stays horizontal and aligned across both frames.
Moss sway remains visible at 4x nearest-neighbor scale without changing the platform width.
